macro.risk_summary: Oil and geopolitical risk jumped hard, but Mexican tourism demand is still holding up and local pump prices remain relatively stable.

metrics.label: Brent Crude
metrics.note: WTI is around $111.29/bbl. The Iran war and effective Strait of Hormuz disruption have repriced oil fast.

metrics.label: USD/MXN
metrics.note: Peso has softened from roughly 17.67 in early March as risk aversion rose, but it is not in disorder.

metrics.label: Mexico Inflation
metrics.note: Latest INEGI print for the first half of March shows inflation re-accelerating, led by food and services pressure.

metrics.label: Gasoline (MX)
metrics.note: National gasoline average is about MXN 21.48/L and diesel about MXN 21.35/L. Pump prices have stayed much calmer than crude so far.

metrics.label: Tourism Demand
metrics.note: Cancun hotel occupancy opened 2026 near 88% and broader Riviera Maya/Tulum late-Feb to early-March signals stayed high, even while Tulum airport routes were cut.

metrics.label: Geopolitical Risk
metrics.note: Iran conflict, shipping disruption, and tighter sanctions are now the main macro threat to fuel, freight, and sentiment.

geopolitical.title: Iran conflict has effectively disrupted Strait of Hormuz traffic
geopolitical.summary: The BBC reports Iran has effectively blocked the Strait of Hormuz since the late-February US-Israel attack, keeping oil transit risk elevated.

geopolitical.title: US policy and sanctions pressure remain live through April
geopolitical.summary: CRS notes active US policy moves around Iran, including sanctions and the Chabahar carve-out timeline ending later in April, which keeps trade uncertainty high.

geopolitical.title: Energy markets shifting into sustained uncertainty
geopolitical.summary: Atlantic Council says the Iran war and effective closure of Hormuz are disrupting oil and gas flows and forcing broader energy-policy shifts.

macro.bottom_line: For Lagoon: do not wait for retail fuel prices to catch up. Tighten pricing, protect margins on ATV/UTV deliveries, and bake a fuel-risk buffer into this week's quotes. For Oaxaca build: keep diesel-heavy hauling and bulk materials on a short leash, get supplier quotes refreshed now, and avoid locking major transport-dependent spend until this oil spike either cools or proves sticky.

ofm.title: 2026 platform crackdown is real
ofm.summary: Biometric verification + AI detection tools now active; violators seeing 30-40% revenue drops. DMs still drive 60-70% of income and AI chatbots can't match human emotional connection. [b9-agency.com](https://b9-agency.com/blog/how-to-create-ai-onlyfans-model-2026)

ofm.title: OnlyMonster emerges as category leader
ofm.summary: Rapidly become the go-to management system/desktop browser for OF agencies, outpacing competitors through Beta with AI assistance integration. [onlymonster.ai](https://onlymonster.ai/)

ofm.title: Fanvue hits $100M run rate
ofm.summary: Raised $22M Series A (Feb 2026), 20,000 new creators joined last month. Platform fee is 20% on $12.50 average subscription. Enhanced AI agents for fan interactions coming. [businesswire.com](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260112458975/en/)

ofm.title: AI influencer revenue benchmarks
ofm.summary: Top virtual influencers (Lil Miquela, Aitana Lopez) earning $1K-$2M per post/year. Successful Fanvue AI creators typically hit $500-5,000/month after 3-6 months consistent posting. [success.com](https://www.success.com/how-ai-influencers-generate-millions-per-year)

ofm.title: Sozee + Supercreator combo
ofm.summary: Agencies pairing Sozee (content generation) with Supercreator (chat/management) + Zapier/Gumloop hitting 50%+ revenue growth through full-funnel automation. [sozee.ai](https://sozee.ai/resources/ai-tools-onlyfans-agencies-2026/)

tools.title: OpenClaw 2026.3.2 released
tools.summary: Major themes = "trustworthy enough for real workflows." New secrets system, safer defaults, PDF tool, task board/status handling. 45 new features, 13 breaking changes, 82 bug fixes in March release. [github.com/openclaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases)

tools.title: Claude Code worktree isolation
tools.summary: Subagents can now use worktree isolation to work in parallel without conflicts. Add `isolation: worktree` to agent frontmatter or ask Claude to "work in a worktree." [code.claude.com/docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/common-workflows)

tools.title: Awesome Claude Code repo
tools.summary: Curated list of skills, hooks, slash-commands, agent orchestrators now maintained at `hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code`. Includes AgentSys workflow automation. [github.com](https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code)

tools.title: Claude Code adaptive learning
tools.summary: Now automatically learns your coding style, project structure, and preferences over time. Scheduled tasks and autonomous agent capabilities expanded. [popularaitools.ai](https://popularaitools.ai/blog/claude-code-scheduled-tasks-autonomous-agents-2026)

tools.title: Multi-agent coordination v4
tools.summary: New features include `fork_context`, `task_name`, `send_message`, `assign_task`, `list_agents` + `spawn_agents_on_csv` for parallel wave execution. [Claude-Code-Workflow repo](https://github.com/catlog22/Claude-Code-Workflow)

market.title: Tulum ATV rental market
market.summary: Lagoon Company still dominant in search results. Competitors now offering incentives like "free tequila bottle with rental" to differentiate. Fat-tire ATVs with 200km+ range becoming standard pitch. [tulumatvrental.com](https://www.tulumatvrental.com/)

market.title: Puerto Escondido real estate window
market.summary: Market entering 2026 with strong fundamentals — growing international/domestic demand, improved infrastructure. Large beachfront lots (1,000-3,000 sqm) are significant value plays vs saturated Tulum/Playa markets. [realestate.puerto-escondido.mx](https://realestate.puerto-escondido.mx/puerto-escondido-real-estate-forecast-next-5-years)

market.title: Rammed earth construction trend
market.summary: Prefabricated rammed earth blocks gaining traction — allows factory casting, quality control, reduced construction time/cost. Baja Blox operating in Baja California Norte. [archello.com](https://archello.com/news/detail-rammed-earth-walls-of-casa-ballena-los-cabos)

market.title: Earth USA 2026 conference
market.summary: Sept 18-20 at Scottish Rite Center, Santa Fe. Covers adobe, rammed earth, compressed earth block. Wider field of interest than previous years. [earthusa.org](https://www.earthusa.org)

market.title: Santerra House precedent
market.summary: Mexican studio Parma Arquitectura built rammed earth weekend home in Valle de Guadalupe for under $80K. Burnt timber + rammed earth combo in wine region shows market acceptance. [newatlas.com](https://newatlas.com/architecture/santerra-house-parma-arquitectura/)
